captions.dual_port_heatmap: Number N_use of consecutive usable DPFI frames across the simulated absolute-detuning and F=P tau plane. Each accepted frame has pre-exposure SNR_5x5 >= 8 and post-exposure condensate depletion no greater than 30%. The black contour encloses points retaining at least 90% of the row maximum; it is a relative screening region, not an uncertainty interval. The labelled conditions are A (F=90 mW us), B (F=150 mW us), C (F=300 mW us), all at 1.5 GHz.
captions.fixed_detuning_cross_section: DPFI sequence length at |Delta|/2pi=1.5 GHz. The dashed curve shows the depletion-limited count N_dep and the solid curve shows N_use after the SNR_5x5 >= 8 requirement. The grey area represents frames rejected by image quality, and the highlighted interval is the relative near-maximum screening region.

criteria.stopping_rule: strict integer; an exposure is accepted only when its pre-exposure image meets the SNR criterion and its post-exposure state remains within the condensate-loss limit

provenance_correction.retrospective_source_snapshot_status: post-generation preserved source/config snapshot; not a clean-generation claim

range_rationale.detuning: 0.75-3.0 GHz retains the reference 1.5 GHz line, covers the detuning range used for the principal screening comparison, and avoids extending the figure into a high-detuning region that would require fluences beyond the displayed experimental range.
range_rationale.fluence: 90-300 mW us brackets the A, B and C conditions of Figure 5.1, including the high-SNR reference at 300 mW us, and retains the intermediate sequence-length maximum.
range_rationale.quality: SNR_5x5 >= 8 is the task-specific spatial-structure criterion motivated by the regenerated ORCA-Fusion Figure 5.1; it is a screening requirement, not a universal visibility threshold.

scope.detector: ORCA-Fusion C14440-20UP Ultra quiet screening scenario with M=10, QE=0.65 and manufacturer-typical sigma_r=0.7 e- rms per physical pixel and readout
scope.disturbance: Version 1 heating plus initial-density reabsorption with 2 E_rec per scattering cycle
scope.faraday: ideal atomic response uses kappa_F=-45/91; apparatus-level polarimetric response remains to be calibrated
